folio.label: 125v
folio.l: Vp on hise othes , and on his seurtee
Graunted hi~ loue , on this condiciouö
That euäe mo , myn honour & renoun~
Were saued , bothe pryue and apert
This is to seyn , that after his desert
I ©af hi~ al myn hert , & al my thougút
God wot and he , andthatandthat other weyes nougút
And toke his herte , in chaunge of myn for ay
But sotú is seide , gon sithen many a day
A trewe wygút and a thef , thynke nat on
¢ And whan he sey the thyngö , so fer I gon
That I had graunted hiö, fully my loue
I swicúIn swicúInswicúa gyse , as I haue seid a boueIn swich532 A space marker separates these two words.Est532n was inserted later as part of a correction, not sure about the ink.ODR
And ©euen hi~ , my trewe herte , as free
As he swore , that he ©af his herte to me
A non this Tigre , ful of doublenesse
Fel on his knees , witú so deuout humblesse
Witú so heigú reuäence , as by his chere
So like a gentil louere , of manere
So rauysshed , as it semed for the ioye
That neuäe Iasou~ , or Paris of Troye
Iasouö certes , ne non other man
Syn lametú was , that alderfirst bygan
To louen two , as wryten folk byforn~
Ne neuäe syn , the first man was born~
Ne coude man , by twenty thou©sand parte
Countrefete the sophimes , of his arte
Ne were worthy , don bokelyn his galoche
There doublenesse or feynyngö , shulde approche
Ne so coude thanke a wygút , as he did me

note.notetxt: 549there is a small cross in the left margin exactly next to this line.ODR

folio.l: His manere was an heuene , for to se
To any wo~man , were she neuäe so wys
So peynted he and kempt , at poynt deuys
As wel hise wordes , as his countenaunce
And so loued hiö , for his obeisaunce
And for the trouthe , I demed in his herte
That if so were , that any thyngö hiö smerte
Al were it neuäe so lite , and I it wiste
Me thougút I felt detú , myn herte twiste
And shortly so ferfortú , this thyngö is wente
That my wyl was , his willes Instrumente
This is to seyn , my wyl obeyed his wille
In alle thynge , as fer as resou~ fille
Kepyngö the boundes , of my worship~ euäe
Ne neuäe hadde I thyngö , so lief ne leuäe
